reasonings.recommended_action: Monitor for trends; no immediate action required.
reasoning_steps: Pressure change is -2.22%, which is within the stable range (<10% change).
Flow rate change is +0.24%, which is negligible and does not indicate a sudden anomaly (>30% increase required for LEAK).
Consumption change is +0.01%, which is stable and does not indicate a spike (>30% increase required for SHORTAGE).
No criteria for LEAK or SHORTAGE are met, so the zone is classified as NORMAL.

reasonings.recommended_action: Monitor for trends; no immediate action required.
reasoning_steps: Pressure change is +4.14% (well below the 20% threshold for LEAK).
Flow rate change is +3.02% (below the 30% threshold for LEAK).
Consumption change is -3.24% (not a spike, so not SHORTAGE).

reasonings.recommended_action: Immediately dispatch repair teams to zone_3 for leak detection and repair. Monitor pressure and flow in real-time for further anomalies.
reasoning_steps: Pressure drop of -34.38% exceeds the 20% threshold for a LEAK classification.
Flow rate increase of +63.72% exceeds the 30% threshold for a LEAK classification.
Consumption change of -3.78% is stable (well below 30% spike), ruling out SHORTAGE.
Pressure drop magnitude (-34.38%) and flow increase magnitude (+63.72%) both indicate high severity.
No conflicting indicators; LEAK is the only applicable classification.
